title: Chemical and related process operatives
description: Execute or adapt procedures and techniques for calibrating measurement devices, by applying knowledge of measurement science, mathematics, physics, chemistry, and electronics, sometimes under the direction of engineering staff. Determine measurement standard suitability for calibrating measurement devices. May perform preventive maintenance on equipment. May perform corrective actions to address identified calibration problems. All engineering technologists and technicians, except drafters, not listed separately. Test the safety of structures, vehicles, or vessels using x-ray, ultrasound, fiber optic or related equipment. (Based on 76 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Monitor transportation and storage of flammable and other potentially dangerous products to ensure safety guidelines are followed.
Read and analyze specifications, schedules, logs, test results, and recommendations to set equipment controls for required product qualities and quantities.
Synchronize activities with other pumphouses to ensure a continuous flow of products and minimize contamination between products.
Patrol units to monitor oil in storage tanks and verify operations are safe, efficient, and comply with regulations.
Maintain and repair equipment, or report malfunctions to supervisors for scheduling repairs.
Collect product samples from tanks or pipelines for quality control testing.
Inspect pipelines and perform routine maintenance tasks, including tightening connections and lubricating valves as necessary.
Conduct general housekeeping of units, including cleaning duties and managing oil spills to ensure compliance with safety regulations.
Perform tests on product samples to assess qualities such as purity, moisture content, and levels of foreign materials using various testing apparatus.
Adjust valves and equipment to achieve specified performance levels and troubleshoot issues by making control adjustments or performing repairs.
Record instrument readings and operational changes, maintaining logs of operational data and test results.
Start and stop equipment, including pumps and compressors, while regulating flow and pressure through control panels.
Monitor and control auxiliary equipment, including pumps, fans, and compressors, to supply necessary utilities for plant operations.
Collaborate with other operators to diagnose and resolve unit problems, ensuring efficient operations across systems.
Analyze and respond to abnormal conditions, implementing corrective actions for safety and efficiency in operations.
Maintain operational logs and records for equipment performance, including outputs and compliance with safety standards.
Operate equipment to regulate processes in the manufacturing of biofuels or biomass-based power, ensuring specifications are followed.
Clean, lubricate, and adjust control devices and equipment to ensure proper functioning and prevent breakdowns.
Coordinate major projects and shutdowns effectively to minimize disruptions to operations.
Implement operational procedures for starting and stopping plants or units during schedule changes or emergencies.
Control, monitor, or operate equipment that regulates or distributes electricity or steam, using data obtained from instruments or computers.
Coordinate with engineers, planners, and field personnel to provide information such as clearances, switching orders, or distribution process changes.
Monitor and record switchboard or control board readings to ensure that electrical or steam distribution equipment is operating properly.
Calculate load estimates or equipment requirements to determine required control settings; apply knowledge of operational data and energy schedules.
Track conditions that could affect power needs and adjust equipment accordingly, such as changes in weather.
Inspect operational equipment and systems to ensure they meet specifications and detect any defects or malfunctions.
Start or stop power generating equipment, including boilers, turbines, or generators, based on operational needs or emergencies.
Repair or replace damaged equipment and perform preventive maintenance to ensure optimal operation.
Test materials and solutions using testing equipment and perform corrective actions for identified problems.
Fill out required job or shift report forms detailing operational tasks and observations.
Monitor gauges, flowmeters, and inspect equipment to ensure that tank levels, temperatures, chemical amounts, and pressures are specified, reporting abnormalities as necessary.
Connect hoses and pipelines to pumps and vessels prior to material transfer using hand tools.
Measure and mix chemicals to prepare solutions for processing according to formulas, and add dyes, water, detergents, or chemicals to tanks as needed.
Maintain records of processing quantities, types, materials used, and customer charges.
Check equipment to ensure it is operating properly, and notify supervisors or mechanics of any malfunctions.
Observe machine operations, control boards, and gauges to detect malfunctions and ensure products meet specifications, making adjustments as necessary.
Operate machines and equipment to wash, bleach, dye, or process fabric, ensuring they function correctly and meet specifications.
Examine and feel products to identify defects and variations from processing standards.
Start and control machines to extrude and form filaments from synthetic materials, adjusting settings as needed.
Weigh products or estimate their weight and grade them according to factors such as color, species, length, and quality for proper processing and usage.
Clean and maintain equipment, such as photoprocessing machines and extruding machines, using appropriate tools and solutions.
Remove inferior or defective products or foreign matter from processed items, discarding as necessary.
Record details of machine malfunctions and production data, noting operating conditions and outputs.
Load materials and solutions into processing machines, maintaining appropriate feed rates and handling specifics as required by operation standards.
Confer with colleagues to address workflow issues or to obtain necessary information regarding processing plans or order details.
Inspect products to verify that they meet specifications and identify whether machine adjustment is needed.
Replace depleted supply packages with full packages and manage inventory of supplies received and consumed.
Flush or clean equipment, and drain materials to maintain operational efficiency.
Make minor repairs, lubricate, and maintain equipment using hand tools to ensure proper functioning.
Mix or blend chemicals or ingredients according to specified formulas during processing.
Add treating agents to products and pump them through filters to remove impurities or precipitate products.
Monitor equipment operation, reporting any malfunctions or deviations to supervisors.
Collect and prepare samples of materials or products for analysis and testing.
Record operational data such as temperatures, pressures, and test results for analysis and review.
Test samples for specific properties to ensure compliance with quality standards or specifications.
Operate and control equipment used for processing materials, ensuring compliance with safety regulations.
Observe work areas and monitor equipment to ensure safety and efficiency in operations.
Adjust machine controls to regulate processing conditions such as temperature and pressure based on procedures.
Direct activities of workers assisting in the manufacturing processes or handling materials.
Describe work assignments and production specifications as outlined in work orders and instructions.
Remove substandard materials from machines to meet product quality standards.
Test machinery functionality to ensure reliable operations in processing activities.
Store materials and products appropriately after processing for shipment or further operations.
Prepare detailed logs of instrument readings and test results for documentation and quality control.
Utilize hand tools and equipment to efficiently dislodge or clear jammed materials from machinery.
Operate and maintain laser plate-making equipment that converts electronic data to plates without the use of film.
Maintain, adjust, and clean equipment, and perform minor repairs, including cleaning tanks and lubricating moving parts of conveyors.
Enter, store, and retrieve information on computer-aided equipment, and input production job settings into workstation terminals.
Maintain production records and monitor inventory levels on a regular basis, ordering additional supplies as necessary.
Measure and set stops, rolls, brushes, and guides on automatic feeders and conveying equipment or coating machines, using tools.
Remove objects from solutions at periodic intervals and observe objects to verify conformance to specifications, inspecting for quality.
Dump, pour, or load specified amounts of refined or unrefined materials into equipment or containers for processing or storage.
Monitor material flow or instruments, such as temperature and pressure gauges, to ensure optimal processing conditions and adjust controls when necessary.
Clean or sterilize tanks, screens, inflow pipes, production areas, or equipment using appropriate methods and tools.
Inspect machines or equipment for hazards, operating efficiency, malfunctions, wear, or leaks and notify supervisors of needed repairs.
Collect samples of materials or products for laboratory analysis and perform tests to determine viscosity, acidity, or other properties using test equipment.
Observe gauges to ensure that machines are operating properly, making adjustments or stopping machines when problems occur to ensure compliance with specifications.
Collect samples of materials or products for laboratory testing and perform routine laboratory tests or analyses to assess quality.
Monitor and adjust operations of geothermal power plant equipment or systems and tend accessory equipment, such as pumps and conveyors.
Inspect, test, or measure materials, products, installations, or work for conformance to specifications and record inspection or test data.
Calibrate liquid flow devices and meters, including fuel, chemical, and water meters, and make minor adjustments to equipment to calibrate instruments.
Perform routine maintenance on mechanical, electrical, or electronic equipment or instruments used in processing and ensure compliance with safety regulations.
Determine whether emergency or auxiliary systems will be needed to keep properties heated or cooled in extreme weather conditions and calculate heat loss and heat gain factors.
Monitor batch, continuous flow, or hybrid biofuels production processes and monitor stored biofuels products or secondary by-products.
Test samples of materials or products to ensure compliance with specifications, using appropriate test equipment and analyze test data to determine results.
Read work orders to determine production specifications, and prepare and maintain logs, reports, or other documentation of work performed.
Identify and correct malfunctions of geothermal plant equipment, electrical systems, instrumentation, or controls and recommend necessary corrective actions.
Lay out mounting holes using measuring instruments, and drill holes with a power drill.
Assemble machines and fasten them to foundations or other structures using tools.
Clean machines and machine parts using cleaning solvents, cloths, air guns, or vacuums.
Observe and test the operation of machinery to diagnose malfunctions using testing devices.
Operate newly repaired machinery to verify repair adequacy and adjust parts as needed.
Analyze test results and information from operators to diagnose equipment problems.
Record details of repairs and maintenance performed, including parts or materials used.
Dismantle defective machines for repair using hand tools, and remove or replace components.
Set up and operate machines, adjusting controls to regulate operations and maintain efficiency.
Inspect or test damaged machine parts and advise supervisors of repair needs or mark defects.
Transport machine parts, tools, and equipment using cranes, hoists, or dollies between work areas.
Maintain and reorder supplies of tools, equipment, and materials as needed for operations.
Verify depths and dimensions of cuts or carvings to ensure adherence to specifications, using measuring instruments.
Study machining instructions, job orders, or blueprints to determine dimensional or finish specifications and tooling requirements.
Clean work area and maintain machines including lubrication and minor repairs.
Operate, set up, and adjust machinery for precision cutting and shaping of materials to specified tolerances.
Use measuring instruments such as calipers, micrometers, or gauges to inspect and ensure conformance of machined work to specifications.
Select and set cutting speeds, feed rates, and depths of cuts based on machining instructions or metal properties.
Position and secure workpieces on machines using clamps, jigs, or other holding devices.
Verify conformance with specifications during machining operations and make necessary adjustments to correct problems.
Mount and position tools in machine chucks, spindles, or other tool holding devices, using hand tools.
Remove workpieces from machines and check to ensure conformity to specifications, using precision measuring instruments.
Repair or replace worn machine parts, using hand tools, or notify engineering personnel when corrective action is required.
Measure and inspect finished workpieces for defects and sort defective pieces by types of flaws.
Program computer numerical control machines for production according to specifications.
Lift and position workpieces using hoists or manually, ensuring they are secure for machining.
Select and install cutting tools and other machine accessories according to specifications, using hand tools or power tools.
Observe machine operations to detect malfunctions and verify that setups conform to specifications.
Establish reference points on workpieces for machining operations to ensure accurate cutting and shaping.
Adjust machine controls according to product specifications using knowledge of operations and settings needed.
Study blueprints or work orders to determine specifications required for machining operations.
Lift and position materials safely for machining operations, ensuring proper securing with fixtures or clamps.
Record production output and maintain operational data, such as time, production, and cost records.
Turn valves or pull levers to start and regulate the flow of coolant or lubricant to work areas.
Set up, operate, or tend machines to saw, cut, shear, slit, punch, crimp, notch, bend, or straighten metal or plastic material.
Start machines, monitor their operations, and record operational data to ensure they conform to specifications.
Measure completed workpieces and raw materials to verify conformance to specifications, using measuring tools such as micrometers and gauges.
Chip slag from linings of ladles or remove linings, and reline or repair ladles and pouring spouts with refractory clay.
Mix specified amounts of sand, clay, mortar powder, and water to form refractory clay or mortar.
Conduct calibration tests to determine performance or reliability of measurement devices and mechanical equipment, and analyze test data.
Calibrate devices and test their specifications by comparing measurements of pressure, temperature, humidity, or environmental conditions to known standards.
Inspect materials, products, or equipment to detect defects or malfunctions and enforce safety and sanitation regulations.
Document non-destructive testing processes and results, including calibration of equipment used in testing.
Supervise the work of other employees engaged in the production or processing of goods, and conduct employee training in procedures and equipment operations.
Monitor machine cycles and operation to identify issues and ensure adherence to production standards.
Perform minor repairs and maintenance on equipment and machinery to ensure operational functionality.
Prepare reports on production results, maintenance activities, and calibration tests.
